english: white mustard, yuṅs-nág (yungs nag) black mustard; yuṅs-°brú (yungs 'bru) grain of mustard-seed, yuṅs-bru tsam (yungs bru tsam) as small as a grain of mustard-seed S.g.; yuṅs-már (yungs mar) oil of mustard.

1. rarely yug (yug), a very small portion of time, moment, acc. to S.g. = कला, stated to be a space of time varying from 8 seconds to 2 1/2 minutes; yúd tsam (yud tsam) (źig) (zhig), yud ré (yud re) but one moment, yúd-tsam-pa (yud tsam pa) Do. of a moment's duration; tse °di yud tsam yin ṗyí-ma-la mta-méd (tshe 'di yud tsam yin phyi ma la mtha' med) this life is but like a moment, the future without end; yúd-kyis (yud kyis), yúd-du (yud du) in a moment, e.g. gnás-su ṗyín-pa (gnas su phyin pa) to get to a place S.g.; for a moment, nám-mk̄a-la ltá-ba (nam mkha' la lta ba) looking up to heaven Wdn. - 2. acc. to Stg. k̄u (khu), fol. 53, yud (yud) is a space of time of longer duration, 48 minutes; acc. to Schr. in Bhot. = ču-tsód (chu tshod) 24 minutes. - 3. a black or coloured stripe on woven fabrics, yud-čan (yud can) striped, black or white W.
Sch.: yud-yúd brid-pa (yud yud brid pa) a dim and indistinct glimmering before one's eyes.

time, when denoting a certain space or length of time, klog kyug-pai yun tsam ma lón-par der ṗyin-nas (klog kyug pa'i yun tsam ma lon par der phyin nas) Mil. in no longer time than a flash of lightning takes he arrived there; yun riṅ-po (yun ring po), W. *-mo* (-mo), a long time, yun riṅ-por (yun ring por), yun riṅ-du (yun ring du) during a long time, yun riṅ-po-nas (yun ring po nas) a long time since or past; *yun máṅ-po bul ču dug* (yun mang po bul chu dug) W. a long time passes; *yun riṅ-ṅi ḳá-na* (yun ring ngi kha na) W. by degrees, gradually; yún-du (yun du) Glr. for a long time to come; yun či srid-du (yun ci srid du) how long? yun túṅ-ba (yun thung ba) a short time.

resp. for ma (ma), 1. mother, btsún-mo yum (btsun mo yum), yum btsún-mo (yum btsun mo) the queen mother. - 2. Ssk. मातृका, title of the third and latest part of the sacred writings, which contains the Abhidharma, or metaphysical portion (Köpp. I, 595. Burn. I, 48): Sch. mentions also an extract of it, yum-čúṅ (yum chung).
only W. to strew, salt on food, ashes on the snow.

I. vb. 1. to slumber, W. also *tom yur-če* (thom yur ce). - 2. v. yúr-ma (yur ma). II. sbst. aqueduct, conduit, water-course, ditch Glr.; yúr-po čē (yur po che) a large trench, channel, canal, yur-ṗráṅ (yur phrang) a small one; sbubs-yur (sbubs yur) a covered, subterranean canal Cs.; yur(-bai) ču (yur ba'i chu) water conveyed by a canal.
the act of weeding C., W.; *yur-ma yur-wa* (yur ma yur wa), C., W. also *čo-če* (co ce) to pull out weeds; metaph. to purify the mind, cleanse the heart, e.g. by disburdening one's conscience.

1. place, a. an inhabited place, as opp. to desolation, ťaṅ stoṅ-pa mi daṅ yul med-čiṅ (thang stong pa mi dang yul med cing) Pth. a desert in which there are neither men nor dwelling-places; b. place, with reference to a sacred community (college, monastery etc.) near it, e.g. some of the students live in the college, others in the place: so yul-dgón (yul dgon) village and monastery, yul-dgón-rnams (yul dgon rnams) Mil. for yúl-mi daṅ dgón-pa-pa-rnams (yul mi dang dgon pa pa rnams) laymen and clerics. c. place, province, country, in a gen. sense, yúl-(gyi) skad (yul gyi skad) provincial dialect, provincialism; yul-(gyi) mtil (yul gyi mthil), mčóg (mchog) chief place, capital; yul čen-po brgyad (yul chen po brgyad) chief places; as such are enumerated in Pth., without any regard to geography, Singhala, Thogar, Li, Balpo, Kashmir, Zahor, Urgyan, Magata; rgya-gar(-gyi) yul (rgya gar gyi yul) India; rgyá-yul (rgya yul), bód-yul (bod yul), sóg-yul (sog yul) India (or China), Tibet, Mongolia; whenever yul precedes a word, as in *yul wa-ra-ṇa-sér* (yul wa ra Na ser), it is to be understood in this way: as to the place (situation), in Banāras; skyíd-yul (skyid yul) a lucky place, sdug-yul (sdug yul) an unlucky one; pá-yul (pha yul) fatherland, native country, home; raṅ-yul (rang yul) one's own country, gźán-yul (gzhan yul) a foreign country; °bróg-yul ('brog yul) country consisting of steppes, roṅ-yul (rong yul) country full of ravines; lha(i)-yul (lha'i yul) land of gods, abode of the lha (lha) also fig., a particularly pleasant country or scenery; mí(i)-yul (mi'i yul) abode of men, (ἡ οἰκουμένη) the inhabited world, earth, yet in the Tibetan sense always as opp. to the abodes of good or evil deities; mii yúl-na mi °oṅ (mi'i yul na mi 'ong) Glr. in the world such a thing is not to be found; rnám-śes dbáṅ-poi yúl-las °dás-pa (rnam shes dbang po'i yul las 'das pa) Wdn. the soul that has left the ex-
